{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Front Desk Officer / Operations Assistant",
  "description": "Ibadan Business School (RC: 972642) was incorporated on the 17th day of August, 2011 under the Company and Allied Matters Act 1990 of the Federal Republic of Nigeria with the primary objective of promoting the growth and advancement of business education within and outside Africa. The School is also licensed by the Centre for Management Development (CMD) of Nigeria- the operational arm of the Nigerian Council for Management Development (NCMD) which is the statutory body charged with the responsibility of regulating the standards of Training and Development Institutions in Nigeria.


We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer / Operations Assistant


Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	The Front Desk Officer / Operations Assistant serves as the first point of contact for Ibadan Business School (IBS) and provides essential administrative, operational, and client relationship support.
	The role involves managing front desk activities, supporting office systems and documentation, assisting programme coordination and reporting, and contributing to marketing and client engagement efforts&mdash;especially for IBS trainings and project-related activities.


Key Responsibilities
Front Desk &amp; Office Administration:


	Welcome and attend to walk-in clients professionally, provide accurate information, and direct enquiries appropriately.
	Receive and manage incoming calls, take messages, and route requests to relevant officers.
	Schedule appointments as approved by the President and ensure adherence to the schedule.
	Verify identification and sign in guests in line with management guidance.
	Receive, log, distribute, and dispatch mails and documents; coordinate pickups and deliveries.
	Maintain accurate registers (attendance, movement logbooks, incoming/outgoing document records) and submit weekly summaries.
	Track and record generator usage and other operational logs as assigned.
	Ensure complaints and concerns are handled promptly and escalated when necessary.
	Stay informed about relevant opportunities/signage and communicate requirements to management.
	Assist with preparing and sending Calls for Nominations (CFNs) for IBS programmes.


Secretarial &amp; Systems Support:


	Prepare computer systems and office equipment for daily operations (printers, documents, templates, files).
	Troubleshoot basic system/equipment issues, document faults in a logbook, and escalate repairs appropriately.
	Support users with basic administrative and system-related requests.
	Maintain strict confidentiality of official information and protect the school&rsquo;s image and integrity.
	Support Operations with training documentation, records, modules preparation, and other assigned administrative tasks.


Marketing &amp; Client Relationship Support:


	Support marketing of IBS programmes to public sector institutions, donor-assisted projects, and private sector clients.
	Help implement outreach plans, identify potential clients, and maintain client engagement records.
	Support relationship management activities, including communication, follow-ups, and responding to enquiries.
	Assist in monitoring outreach performance toward monthly participant and revenue targets.
	Contribute to market scanning and identification of new opportunities and prospective clients.


Operations / Project Support:


	Support the planning and delivery of IBS programmes and field activities when required.
	Assist with stakeholder engagement, advocacy, mobilization, and coordination meetings.
	Prepare workplans, activity schedules, stakeholder databases, and resource tracking documents.
	Maintain accurate records for programme activities and generate timely reports (daily/weekly/monthly).
	Document lessons learned and best practices for future programme improvement.
	Carry out other duties as assigned by the President/CEO or authorised representatives.


Qualifications


	Minimum of B.Sc. in Administration, Social Sciences, Management, or related field.
	Previous experience in front desk, administrative support, operations, or customer service is an advantage.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong communication and interpersonal skills; professional front desk etiquette.
	Excellent organisation, attention to detail, and record-keeping ability.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and email communication.
	Ability to handle multiple tasks, prioritise effectively, and meet deadlines.
	Basic troubleshooting ability for office equipment and routine system issues.
	Confidentiality, integrity, and strong sense of responsibility.
	Willingness to support marketing/outreach and occasional field/project activities.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;jobs.evergreenrecruitment@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;Application for Front Desk Officer / Operations Assistant Role&quot; as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-18",

Front Desk Officer / Operations Assistant Job at Ibadan Business School




        
    

        
        Ibadan Business School (RC: 972642) was incorporated on the 17th day of August, 2011 under the Company and Allied Matters Act 1990 of the Federal Republic of Nigeria with the primary objective of promoting the growth and advancement of business education within and outside Africa. The School is also licensed by the Centre for Management Development (CMD) of Nigeria- the operational arm of the Nigerian Council for Management Development (NCMD) which is the statutory body charged with the responsibility of regulating the standards of Training and Development Institutions in Nigeria.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer / Operations Assistant


Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	The Front Desk Officer / Operations Assistant serves as the first point of contact for Ibadan Business School (IBS) and provides essential administrative, operational, and client relationship support.
	The role involves managing front desk activities, supporting office systems and documentation, assisting programme coordination and reporting, and contributing to marketing and client engagement efforts—especially for IBS trainings and project-related activities.


Key Responsibilities
Front Desk & Office Administration:


	Welcome and attend to walk-in clients professionally, provide accurate information, and direct enquiries appropriately.
	Receive and manage incoming calls, take messages, and route requests to relevant officers.
	Schedule appointments as approved by the President and ensure adherence to the schedule.
	Verify identification and sign in guests in line with management guidance.
	Receive, log, distribute, and dispatch mails and documents; coordinate pickups and deliveries.
	Maintain accurate registers (attendance, movement logbooks, incoming/outgoing document records) and submit weekly summaries.
	Track and record generator usage and other operational logs as assigned.
	Ensure complaints and concerns are handled promptly and escalated when necessary.
	Stay informed about relevant opportunities/signage and communicate requirements to management.
	Assist with preparing and sending Calls for Nominations (CFNs) for IBS programmes.


Secretarial & Systems Support:


	Prepare computer systems and office equipment for daily operations (printers, documents, templates, files).
	Troubleshoot basic system/equipment issues, document faults in a logbook, and escalate repairs appropriately.
	Support users with basic administrative and system-related requests.
	Maintain strict confidentiality of official information and protect the school’s image and integrity.
	Support Operations with training documentation, records, modules preparation, and other assigned administrative tasks.


Marketing & Client Relationship Support:


	Support marketing of IBS programmes to public sector institutions, donor-assisted projects, and private sector clients.
	Help implement outreach plans, identify potential clients, and maintain client engagement records.
	Support relationship management activities, including communication, follow-ups, and responding to enquiries.
	Assist in monitoring outreach performance toward monthly participant and revenue targets.
	Contribute to market scanning and identification of new opportunities and prospective clients.


Operations / Project Support:


	Support the planning and delivery of IBS programmes and field activities when required.
	Assist with stakeholder engagement, advocacy, mobilization, and coordination meetings.
	Prepare workplans, activity schedules, stakeholder databases, and resource tracking documents.
	Maintain accurate records for programme activities and generate timely reports (daily/weekly/monthly).
	Document lessons learned and best practices for future programme improvement.
	Carry out other duties as assigned by the President/CEO or authorised representatives.


Qualifications


	Minimum of B.Sc. in Administration, Social Sciences, Management, or related field.
	Previous experience in front desk, administrative support, operations, or customer service is an advantage.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Strong communication and interpersonal skills; professional front desk etiquette.
	Excellent organisation, attention to detail, and record-keeping ability.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and email communication.
	Ability to handle multiple tasks, prioritise effectively, and meet deadlines.
	Basic troubleshooting ability for office equipment and routine system issues.
	Confidentiality, integrity, and strong sense of responsibility.
	Willingness to support marketing/outreach and occasional field/project activities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Application for Front Desk Officer / Operations Assistant Role" as the subject of the mail.

  "title": "Site Engineer (Agrofield)",
  "description": "Reftop Homes is a diversified investment company operating across Real Estate, Agro-Investment, and Transportation, focused on building secure, long-term assets. We emphasize transparency, proper documentation, and professional project management to protect capital and deliver sustainable value. Through initiatives such as Palm Crest, we integrate real estate and agriculture to enhance asset productivity and long-term performance. We partner with individuals and institutions seeking structured investments, clear governance, and disciplined growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Site Engineer (Agrofield)

Job Location:&nbsp;Abeokuta, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a competent and detail-oriented Site Engineer to oversee and manage on-site activities for our Agrofield project in Abeokuta.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring that construction and agricultural infrastructure development are executed efficiently, safely, and in compliance with approved designs and specifications.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise day-to-day site operations and ensure project execution aligns with approved plans and timelines
	Interpret and implement engineering drawings and technical specifications
	Coordinate with contractors, artisans, and project stakeholders on-site
	Monitor project progress and provide regular status updates to management
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and environmental standards
	Conduct site inspections and resolve technical issues as they arise
	Manage materials usage and ensure proper documentation of site activities
	Support quality control processes to maintain project standards
	Assist in cost control by minimizing waste and ensuring efficient resource utilization


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Civil Engineering, Agricultural Engineering, or related field
	2 &ndash; 5 years of relevant experience in construction or agro-infrastructure projects
	Strong understanding of site operations and project management principles
	Ability to read and interpret technical drawings
	Good problem-solving and decision-making skills
	Excellent communication and team coordination abilities
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and basic engineering tools/software.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@reftophomes.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Site Engineer (Agrofield) Job at Reftop Homes Limited




        
    

        
        Reftop Homes is a diversified investment company operating across Real Estate, Agro-Investment, and Transportation, focused on building secure, long-term assets. We emphasize transparency, proper documentation, and professional project management to protect capital and deliver sustainable value. Through initiatives such as Palm Crest, we integrate real estate and agriculture to enhance asset productivity and long-term performance. We partner with individuals and institutions seeking structured investments, clear governance, and disciplined growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Site Engineer (Agrofield)

Job Location: Abeokuta, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	We are seeking a competent and detail-oriented Site Engineer to oversee and manage on-site activities for our Agrofield project in Abeokuta.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring that construction and agricultural infrastructure development are executed efficiently, safely, and in compliance with approved designs and specifications.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise day-to-day site operations and ensure project execution aligns with approved plans and timelines
	Interpret and implement engineering drawings and technical specifications
	Coordinate with contractors, artisans, and project stakeholders on-site
	Monitor project progress and provide regular status updates to management
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and environmental standards
	Conduct site inspections and resolve technical issues as they arise
	Manage materials usage and ensure proper documentation of site activities
	Support quality control processes to maintain project standards
	Assist in cost control by minimizing waste and ensuring efficient resource utilization


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering, Agricultural Engineering, or related field
	2 – 5 years of relevant experience in construction or agro-infrastructure projects
	Strong understanding of site operations and project management principles
	Ability to read and interpret technical drawings
	Good problem-solving and decision-making skills
	Excellent communication and team coordination abilities
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and basic engineering tools/software.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "Reftop Homes is a diversified investment company operating across Real Estate, Agro-Investment, and Transportation, focused on building secure, long-term assets. We emphasize transparency, proper documentation, and professional project management to protect capital and deliver sustainable value. Through initiatives such as Palm Crest, we integrate real estate and agriculture to enhance asset productivity and long-term performance. We partner with individuals and institutions seeking structured investments, clear governance, and disciplined growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Sangotedo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
Financial Management:


	Maintain accurate financial records of all company transactions.
	Prepare monthly financial reports for management review.
	Monitor income and expenses across real estate projects.
	Prepare cash flow reports and financial summaries.
	Maintain proper documentation of financial transactions.


Property Sales &amp; Client Accounts:


	Record and track client payments for land and property purchases.
	Monitor installment payment plans and outstanding balances.
	Issue receipts and payment confirmations to clients.
	Reconcile sales records with bank transactions.
	Maintain accurate client financial records.


Banking &amp; Reconciliation:


	Perform regular bank reconciliations.
	Monitor company bank accounts and transactions.
	Investigate and resolve discrepancies.
	Prepare payment schedules for vendors and contractors.


Accounts &amp; Payroll:


	Process staff salaries and maintain payroll records.
	Manage accounts payable and receivable.
	Track operational expenses.
	Maintain vendor and contractor payment records.


Tax &amp; Compliance:


	Prepare and remit statutory deductions (PAYE, Pension, NHF, NSITF).
	Assist with tax filing and compliance.
	Maintain proper records for audit purposes.
	Ensure compliance with financial policies and regulations.


Reporting:


	Prepare weekly and monthly financial reports.
	Provide financial data to management for decision-making.
	Maintain organized financial files and records.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related fields
	Minimum of 2 - 4 years accounting experience.
	Knowledge of financial reporting and bookkeeping.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
	Good analytical and organizational skills.


Preferred Qualifications:


	ICAN or ACCA (or in view)
	Experience handling property sales transactions.
	Experience with installment payment tracking.
	Knowledge of Nigerian tax regulations.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@reftophomes.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Accountant Job at Reftop Homes Limited




        
    

        
        Reftop Homes is a diversified investment company operating across Real Estate, Agro-Investment, and Transportation, focused on building secure, long-term assets. We emphasize transparency, proper documentation, and professional project management to protect capital and deliver sustainable value. Through initiatives such as Palm Crest, we integrate real estate and agriculture to enhance asset productivity and long-term performance. We partner with individuals and institutions seeking structured investments, clear governance, and disciplined growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Sangotedo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Key Responsibilities
Financial Management:


	Maintain accurate financial records of all company transactions.
	Prepare monthly financial reports for management review.
	Monitor income and expenses across real estate projects.
	Prepare cash flow reports and financial summaries.
	Maintain proper documentation of financial transactions.


Property Sales & Client Accounts:


	Record and track client payments for land and property purchases.
	Monitor installment payment plans and outstanding balances.
	Issue receipts and payment confirmations to clients.
	Reconcile sales records with bank transactions.
	Maintain accurate client financial records.


Banking & Reconciliation:


	Perform regular bank reconciliations.
	Monitor company bank accounts and transactions.
	Investigate and resolve discrepancies.
	Prepare payment schedules for vendors and contractors.


Accounts & Payroll:


	Process staff salaries and maintain payroll records.
	Manage accounts payable and receivable.
	Track operational expenses.
	Maintain vendor and contractor payment records.


Tax & Compliance:


	Prepare and remit statutory deductions (PAYE, Pension, NHF, NSITF).
	Assist with tax filing and compliance.
	Maintain proper records for audit purposes.
	Ensure compliance with financial policies and regulations.


Reporting:


	Prepare weekly and monthly financial reports.
	Provide financial data to management for decision-making.
	Maintain organized financial files and records.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related fields
	Minimum of 2 - 4 years accounting experience.
	Knowledge of financial reporting and bookkeeping.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
	Good analytical and organizational skills.


Preferred Qualifications:


	ICAN or ACCA (or in view)
	Experience handling property sales transactions.
	Experience with installment payment tracking.
	Knowledge of Nigerian tax regulations.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
